title: The smartest surrounds himself with smarter — leadership maxim (2019)
html:
The smartest surrounds himself with smarter — leadership maxim (2019)

Source: 2019-10-22 Apple Note. Three-line note: a leadership maxim, a flirtation rule, and a paternal warning.

TL;DR

  • "The smartest isn't the smartest in the room — the smartest is the one who surrounds himself with the smartest and gets them to follow him" — early formulation of the mentors-from-afar / surround-yourself-with-better thesis. Recurs in Ahmad Jamal stream (Steve Jobs / Jay-Z principles).
  • "The key to females is making them laugh — by being witty and funny naturally" — HS-era flirtation rule, age ~16.
  • "Give your dad an inch he will take a mile" — earliest written acknowledgment of the boundaries-with-dad pattern that becomes a recurring dad wisdom tension across the 2024 01 dad in bad state arc.
  • Three lines, three domains: leadership / women / family. Compact mind-snapshot of HS-Yahir's mental model.
  • Predates OSU, FBLA state-level activity, and the founder loneliness arc.

Connections

  • dad wisdom
  • 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream — recurrence of surround-yourself principle
  • 2019 08 15 credentials and ceo maxims — companion HS-era maxims note
